title: Informal Conjecture on Universal N0 for Bumping a Ladder
statement_text: There exists a constant $N_0$ such that for every $k$, Theorem 2 holds with $N = N_0$. For all we know, this might even be true with $N_0 = 4$.
context_text: Theorem 2 (Bumping a ladder) asserts that every 3-connected graph containing a union of $N$ vertex-disjoint copies of $L_k$ as a minor contains $L_{k+1}$ as a minor, but the value of $N$ a priori depends on $k$. The authors remark that while 3-connectivity is necessary (see Figure 1), they believe the dependence of $N$ on $k$ is not.
notes: Stated in prose immediately after Theorem 2 with the phrase 'We conjecture'; no labelled environment.

title: Open Problem: Characterization of Unavoidable Graphs
statement_text: Obtain a full characterization of unavoidable graphs, where a graph $H$ is unavoidable if the cover graph of every poset with large enough dimension contains $H$ as a minor.
context_text: The known positive results are that every ladder and $K_4$ are unavoidable. Kelly's construction shows that every unavoidable graph must be a minor of some graph from that construction, providing a necessary condition. The paper's Conjecture 6 proposes that this necessary condition is also sufficient.
notes: Stated in prose as 'it is an open problem'; no labelled environment. Conjecture 6 in the same paper proposes a conjectured answer.

title: Conjecture 6
statement_text: A graph $H$ is unavoidable if and only if $H$ is a minor of some graph from Kelly's construction.
context_text: Kelly's construction gives posets with unbounded dimension whose cover graphs are planar and have pathwidth 3, implying every unavoidable graph must be a minor of some graph in this family. The authors conjecture this necessary condition is also sufficient, and note an equivalent reformulation: $H$ is unavoidable if and only if $H$ is a minor of some graph obtained by gluing copies of $K_4$ along edges in a path-like way and subdividing all horizontal edges once.
